name: Mexican Pork-Garlic Stew with Hominy
recipeIngredient: 3 lb boneless pork shoulder (or butt, cut into 1-inch cubes)
1 quart beef stock (low-salt or beef broth)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pan, sauté the chorizo over moderate heat until it loses its pink color, about 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon.
2. Add onion and garlic to the pan; cook in the chorizo fat over moderate heat for 10 minutes.
3. Stir in sherry and cook until the liquid evaporates.
4. Return the pork and chorizo mixture to the pot.
5. Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot, then brown pork cubes on all sides.
6. Add spices, oregano, and beef stock to the pot; bring to a boil over high heat, scraping the bottom of the pot to remove any particles.
7. Reduce heat to moderate and cook for 2 hours or until meat is tender.
8. Stir in hominy and lime juice; continue cooking for 10 minutes.
9. Mix in green onions and cilantro just before serving. Serve hot over rice.
